name: PopoverFilter
description: A popover-based multi-select filter component with search functionality. Displays a list of checkboxes within a popover, allowing users to filter data by selecting multiple options with real-time search.
usage_guidelines: Use for filtering tables, lists, or data views.
Provide data as array of {label, value} objects.
Handle selection changes via onChange callback.
Use renderTrigger for custom trigger buttons.
Enable showReset to allow clearing all filters.

data.description: Array of filter options with label and value properties.

onChange.type: (data: Array<{ label: string; value: string }>) => void
onChange.description: Callback fired when selection changes. Receives array of selected items.

title.description: Title displayed in the popover header.

placement.description: Popover placement relative to trigger element.

inputPlaceholder.description: Placeholder text for the search input.

showReset.description: Shows 'Clear filters' button when items are selected.

value.description: Controlled value - array of selected option values.

renderTrigger.description: Custom trigger element to open the popover.

width.description: Width of the popover in pixels.

styling_info.visual_description: A popover containing a search input at the top, followed by a vertical list of checkboxes. Each checkbox item has hover highlighting. A 'Clear filters' button appears at the bottom when items are selected. Supports dark mode.

Do: Provide meaningful labels for filter options
Use controlled value prop for external state management
Customize trigger button to match your UI
Dont: Don't use for single-select scenarios (use Select instead)
Don't use with very large datasets without virtualization
Don't forget to handle empty states
Don't hide the reset button for better UX

Basic.title: Basic

CustomTrigger.title: Custom Trigger
